ucs.title: [LLM] Cobalt Group (GOLD KINGSWOOD) Office-exploit chain: EQNEDT32/WINWORD spawning LOLBin downloader for CobInt stager
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Cobalt Group initial-access chain where a weaponized RTF/DOC delivered via spearphishing exploits CVE-2017-11882/CVE-2017-8759 in Microsoft Equation Editor or triggers macro/DDE execution, then spawns a scripting host or LOLBin (regsvr32, mshta, wscript, odbcconf, cmstp) to fetch the CobInt/More_eggs second-stage. This pattern is documented across Cobalt Group bank-targeting campaigns from 2017-2019 against Eastern European, Central Asian and SE Asian financial institutions.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe","powerpnt.exe") Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","odbcconf.exe","cmstp.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval susp=case(match(process,"(?i)(http(s)?://|\\\\\\\\[^\\s]+\\.(sct|hta|js|vbs|ps1|xsl))"),3,match(process,"(?i)(scrobj|/i:|-enc|frombase64|downloadstring|invoke-expression|iex\\b|-nop|-w\\s*hidden)"),2,match(parent_process_name,"eqnedt32\\.exe"),3,1=1,1) | where susp>=2 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | table firstTime lastTime host user parent_process_name process_name process susp
ucs.kql:
let lolbins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","odbcconf.exe","cmstp.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
let officeparents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe","powerpnt.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeparents)
| where FileName in~ (lolbins)
| extend cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| where cmd has_any ("http://","https://","\\\\","scrobj","/i:","-enc","frombase64","downloadstring","iex","invoke-expression",".sct",".hta",".xsl","-w hidden","-nop")
   or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "eqnedt32.exe"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, ReportId
| join kind=leftouter (DeviceNetworkEvents | where RemoteUrl has_any (".top",".club",".pw",".info",".xyz")) on DeviceName
| sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Cobalt Group's documented kill-chain almost exclusively starts with weaponized Office documents (RTF Equation Editor exploits, macro/DDE) followed by a scripting host or signed-binary proxy launching CobInt. Pivoting on the (Office/EQNEDT32 parent) -> (LOLBin) -> (URL/UNC/encoded) triple matches this group's tradecraft far more tightly than a generic Office-spawn rule, while not relying on hashes

ucs.title: [LLM] Cobalt Spider signed-binary proxy execution: regsvr32/odbcconf/cmstp fetching remote scriptlet (CobInt second-stage)
ucs.description: Hunts Cobalt Group's hallmark signed-binary proxy execution where regsvr32 /i:http (Squiblydoo), odbcconf -f or -a regsvr, and cmstp /ni /s with a remote INF are used to load remote SCT/COM scriptlets and stage CobInt or Cobalt Strike beacons. This LOLBin combination is uncommon outside this actor and a small set of imitators, and is repeatedly called out in Group-IB, PTSecurity and Talos reporting on Cobalt Gang.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("regsvr32.exe","odbcconf.exe","cmstp.exe","mshta.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval cmd=lower(process) | eval cobalt_pattern=case( process_name="regsvr32.exe" AND match(cmd,"(?i)/i:.*http|/i:.*\\\\\\\\|scrobj\\.dll"),"squiblydoo", process_name="odbcconf.exe" AND match(cmd,"(?i)(-f\\s|/f\\s|regsvr|-a\\s+\\{regsvr)"),"odbcconf-regsvr", process_name="cmstp.exe" AND match(cmd,"(?i)/ni\\s|/s\\s.*\\.inf|http|\\\\\\\\"),"cmstp-inf", process_name="mshta.exe" AND match(cmd,"(?i)(http|vbscript:|javascript:|\\\\\\\\)"),"mshta-remote", 1=1,null) | where isnotnull(cobalt_pattern) | join type=left host [| tstats summariesonly=t values(All_Traffic.dest) as c2_dest from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app="http" OR All_Traffic.app="https" by All_Traffic.src | rename All_Traffic.src as host] | table firstTime lastTime host user parent_process_name process_name cobalt_pattern process c2_dest
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where FileName in~ ("regsvr32.exe","odbcconf.exe","cmstp.exe","mshta.exe")
| extend cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| extend CobaltPattern = case(
    FileName =~ "regsvr32.exe" and (cmd matches regex @"/i:\s*(https?://|\\\\)" or cmd has "scrobj.dll" or cmd matches regex @"/s\s+/u\s+/i:"), "Squiblydoo",
    FileName =~ "odbcconf.exe" and (cmd has "regsvr" or cmd matches regex @"\s-f\s" or cmd matches regex @"-a\s*\{?\s*regsvr"), "OdbcconfRegsvr",
    FileName =~ "cmstp.exe" and (cmd has "/ni" or cmd has "/s" or cmd has "http" or cmd has "\\\\"), "CmstpRemoteInf",
    FileName =~ "mshta.exe" and (cmd has "http" or cmd has "vbscript:" or cmd has "javascript:" or cmd has "\\\\"), "MshtaRemote",
    "")
| where isnotempty(CobaltPattern)
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (".sct",".hta",".xsl",".inf",".png",".jpg")
          or RemoteUrl matches regex @"/[a-z0-9]{6,}\.(php|asp)"
    | project DeviceName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, NetTime=Timestamp
) on DeviceName
| where abs(datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, NetTime)) < 60 or isempty(NetTime)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, CobaltPattern, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
| sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Cobalt Group is one of the few financially-motivated crews that operationalised the full odbcconf/cmstp/regsvr32 trio for staging CobInt against banks; legitimate admin use of these binaries with /i:http, -f remote INF, or /ni is essentially nil. Correlating the LOLBin invocation with a near-simultaneous outbound HTTP fetch of an SCT/HTA/INF/XSL artifact gives a high-confidence install-stage detec
